items.title: Add truthful provenance labels for every interpreted plan
items.user_problem: A user cannot reliably tell whether a plan came from a reviewed recipe, manual preset, Hermes selection, Hermes authorship, deterministic repair, or a capability gap.
items.evidence: Current UI derives labels from raw source strings such as manual_preset, manual_host_interpreter, and hermes_frontier_agent; it does not expose the required source ontology.
items.proposed_change: Add source enum and trace fields for REVIEWED_RECIPE, MANUAL_PRESET, HERMES_RECIPE_SELECTION, HERMES_NOVEL_COMPOSITION, DETERMINISTIC_REPAIR, and CAPABILITY_GAP. Show a concise label in the interpretation panel and detailed lineage in Developer details.
items.affected_state_screen: Interpreted plan; Developer details; execution provenance
items.acceptance_test: A preset never displays as AI-authored; Hermes recipe selection never displays as novel composition; deterministic fallback displays Safety repair applied; unsupported requests display CAPABILITY_GAP and no plan_document.
items.expected_impact: Prevents the core trust failure of making a fallback or preset look like model authorship.

items.title: Make Hermes model interpretation fail closed and stable
items.user_problem: The UI says Hermes frontier interpretation is ready, but model-mode interpretation currently returns HTTP 500.
items.evidence: Live /api/interpret mode=model returned HTTP 500 for supported, ambiguous, unsupported, and novel candidate prompts.
items.proposed_change: Catch Hermes invocation exceptions and return MODEL_UNAVAILABLE with a trace id; fix the underlying deployment invocation so one supported Hermes journey succeeds.
items.affected_state_screen: Ask Hermes; interpretation loading; error state
items.acceptance_test: Supported recipe, corridor draft, ambiguous support, and unsupported optimality prompts return typed InterpretResponse states with no 500s.
items.expected_impact: Restores trust in Hermes as first-class citizen.

items.title: Prove one true novel composition or remove the claim from the preview
items.user_problem: The product thesis says Hermes authors new tactical definitions, but current product evidence only proves recipe selection and corridor-template drafting.
items.evidence: Compiler supports select_recipe/draft_corridor/clarify/capability_gap; live model mode fails; no UI New experimental composition label.
items.proposed_change: Implement a live proof where Hermes composes at least two capabilities into a non-template plan, binds it, executes it, and labels it New experimental composition.
items.affected_state_screen: Ask Hermes; interpreted plan; result rail; replay
items.acceptance_test: Natural-language hero request makes Hermes create a schema-valid plan structurally different from both registered recipes/templates, combines at least two agent-composable capabilities, uses temporal or relational logic, requires no code/prompt change, produces a new plan hash, starts with cache_status=MISS, and executes honestly over real data.
items.expected_impact: Proves the main product thesis instead of a recipe workbench.

items.title: Block unsupported concepts before preset fallback
items.user_problem: A user can ask for scanning/body-angle intent and receive a corridor plan if a preset is selected.
items.evidence: Manual query 'Infer what the midfielder meant to do from his scanning and body angle' returned PLAN_INTERPRETED for corridor preset.
items.proposed_change: Normalize unsupported gap codes before any preset selection; include scanning, body angle, body shape, intent, optimality, pass probability, communication, and video.
items.acceptance_test: Unsupported prompt variants always return CAPABILITY_GAP and preserve original request; no plan_document is returned.

items.title: Clear stale artifacts on mode, query, and preset changes
items.user_problem: Prior plans/results can remain visible after the user changes the mode or wording that produced them.
items.evidence: Source clears dependent state for scope changes but not for mode/query edits; preset can coexist with unrelated query text.
items.proposed_change: Mark interpretation stale on query edit and clear validation/confirmation/execution/replay on mode or preset change.
items.affected_state_screen: Query panel; interpreted plan; result rail; replay
items.acceptance_test: After post-execution query edit, mode switch, or preset switch, validate/confirm/execute are disabled and old results/replay are hidden until reinterpreted.
items.expected_impact: Eliminates contradictory states and stale evidence risk.

items.title: Add corridor overlay legend and temporal validity
items.user_problem: The replay line looks authoritative but does not say when the corridor exists or what it proves.
items.evidence: PitchCanvas draws ball-to-target whenever both are in frame using target_player_id; open/close frames and limiting defender are not rendered.
items.proposed_change: Project open/close frame evidence, show corridor only during valid interval, add legend and measurements for duration/clearance.
items.acceptance_test: Scrubbing outside relation interval hides/fades corridor; legend includes required non-optimality claim.
items.expected_impact: Makes tactical visualization inspectable and safer.
dependencies: Evidence projection fields for relation open/close

items.title: Simplify default IA into four stages
items.user_problem: The first-time user has to parse host confirmation, validation, raw IDs, evidence aliases, and predicate JSON.
items.evidence: UI shows Host confirm, Submit and validate, browser/API badges, known-timestamp form, raw result/replay ids, predicate ids.
items.proposed_change: Default layout becomes Ask or choose, Understand, Approve and run, Explore moments; move technical material to Developer details.
items.acceptance_test: Default view hides known timestamp, API badges, raw IDs, raw predicate JSON, and raw typed plan while Developer details preserve them.
items.expected_impact: Makes the link understandable without coaching.

items.title: Improve cold execution waiting state
items.user_problem: Cold execution can take minutes without explaining what is happening.
items.evidence: Observed first-run cloud latency: approved 62.631s; corridor 170.909s.
items.proposed_change: Show selected matches, elapsed time, deterministic runtime phase, cache promise, and cancel.
items.acceptance_test: Cold MISS displays elapsed time and selected match count; cancel stops UI wait and preserves validated plan.
items.expected_impact: Reduces abandonment during the slowest path.
dependencies: Cancelable frontend request or cancel-safe UI state

items.title: Replace result-card implementation labels with tactical summaries
items.user_problem: Result rail labels such as RETAINED_NO_SWITCH and PROGRESSIVE_CORRIDOR_AVAILABLE are not self-explanatory.
items.evidence: Result rail renders result.classification.replaceAll('_',' ') as primary copy.
items.proposed_change: Map classifications to concise tactical headlines and show two principal measurements.
items.acceptance_test: Formal classifications appear only in Developer details; visible cards show match, time, tactical event, and measurements.
items.expected_impact: Makes moments scannable for a football expert.
dependencies: Measurement projection for each recipe family

items.title: Guided clarification inputs
items.user_problem: Clarification questions require manual rewriting in the query box.
items.evidence: Clarification state lists questions but no answer controls.
items.proposed_change: Add structured answer chips/fields for support definition, time window, and distance threshold.
items.acceptance_test: Answering clarification submits clarifications array and produces a fresh interpretation.
items.expected_impact: Makes ambiguity resolution feel like a product loop.
dependencies: Interpret API clarifications field in browser

items.title: Add match grouping and filters in result rail
items.user_problem: Large result sets are flat and hard to compare across matches.
items.evidence: Approved run returns 180 total; UI shows returned result list without grouping.
items.proposed_change: Group by match/period and add filters for classification and result source.
items.acceptance_test: User can filter to one match/classification and preserve replay selection coherently.

items.title: Recipe revision and feedback loop
items.user_problem: The intended feedback/revision loop is not yet in the deployed Workbench.
items.evidence: Knowledge pack includes feedback/version tools but browser routes for record_feedback are 404 in Workbench tests.
items.proposed_change: Add labels, semantic diffs, result deltas, and immutable saved recipes after the preview is stable.
items.acceptance_test: User labels a result, Hermes proposes revision, UI shows added/removed/retained moments, user saves version.
items.expected_impact: Moves from demo to analyst workflow.
